subject: v4 migration home map — every data category's home in WAI-Harness/, four-bucket classification, no-orphan invariant, framework-role dissolution
title: v4 Migration Home Map & Framework-Role Dissolution
situation: v4 restructures WAI-Spoke/ into WAI-Harness/{spoke,hub}/ with a managed/(Basher-distributed, read-only) vs local/(spoke-owned) split, advisor co-location, and a dedicated ozi/ root. A two-part audit (S46) inventoried ~3,800 files across ~55 data categories and audited every responsibility the 'framework' concept owned. Removing 'framework' (v4: 'there is no framework — only the harness') redistributes its authority but orphans no role outright. The migration must give EVERY data category an explicit home — no silent orphans — and reshape routing/ownership policy accordingly. Without a canonical map, migration is guesswork and data is silently lost (the exact failure WAI exists to prevent).
why: Two failure modes this spec closes: (1) data orphaned — a category with no declared home is silently dropped or dumped in the wrong place, invisible until a future agent reads stale paths; (2) policy drift — framework's dissolved responsibilities (routing, curation, distribution, infra) land nowhere and the curator/distribution model quietly breaks. The home map is the single source of truth the migration engine reads; the no-orphan invariant is a hard gate (dry-run fails if any live category is unclaimed). This reinforces P1 (nothing survives without explicit save), P2 (verify), and the curator model.
audience: v4 migration engine (tools/v4_migrate.py) — reads this map as its authoritative source; refuses to run if any live category is unmapped
Basher — owns managed/ distribution; this map declares which categories are managed
Trainer (hub) — inherits framework's curation role; this map records that dissolution
Hub — inherits framework's infrastructure automation (base cuts, teaching delivery)
Ozi — owns spoke/ozi/; this map declares Ozi-special consolidation

canonical_master.rule: MyWheel/WAI-Harness/ is the SINGLE distribution master. ALL harness changes (spoke-side or hub-side) must be applied to MyWheel first; Basher distributes from here to every spoke (own-copy, pull on spin-up, Basher upgrades when a newer master exists). A harness change that lands anywhere but MyWheel is not canonical and will be overwritten on next pull.
canonical_master.spoke/: the canonical SPOKE harness blueprint — what every spoke pulls its own copy of
canonical_master.hub/: the canonical HUB harness AND the running hub node (old wheelwright/hub/ decommissioned into here; the hub is singular so master = instance)
canonical_master.spoke_self_dev: MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/local/ is also where the harness develops itself (the v4 epic/specs/lugs migrate here) — it dogfoods its own structure

folder_model.MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/managed/: Basher-distributed harness code; agent-READ-ONLY; replaced on update
folder_model.MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/local/: spoke-owned data + extensions + harness self-dev; never overwritten by updates
folder_model.MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/ozi/: Ozi's consolidated state + identity (live agent at spoke root)
folder_model.MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/advisors/<name: each advisor co-located with the data it owns; each has patterns/
folder_model.MyWheel/WAI-Harness/hub/: canonical hub harness + running hub node; same managed/local split
folder_model.per_spoke_copy: each real spoke (website, track, basher, minder...) holds its OWN copy of WAI-Harness/spoke/ pulled from the master; managed/ is replaced on upgrade, local/ is the spoke's own data

decommission.framework_repo: wheelwright/framework/ becomes irrelevant — its harness-authoring role moves to MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/; its harness-dev data (v4 epic, specs, lugs) migrates to MyWheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/local/; operational cruft goes Flag/Drop; folder trashed on green certification + sign-off
decommission.old_hub_repo: wheelwright/hub/ decommissioned into MyWheel/WAI-Harness/hub/ (registry, advisors, WAI-Hub data migrate); folder trashed on green certification + sign-off
decommission.rule: no legacy folders dragged forward — clean cut once MyWheel is certified green
decommission.destruction_human_gate: CRITICAL (external review B5): a hallucinated-green certification must NOT auto-trigger irreversible decommissioning. Project-level teardown (removing framework/ or old hub/) is a HARD, non-bypassable human gate — an explicit user CLI acknowledgement (mode: human via Wilbur), never cleared by autonomous certification alone. The engine may STAGE assets to trash_bin on green, but actual project deprecation waits for the signed user ack. Staging is reversible; deprecation is gated.

four_bucket_rules.Preserve: valid data, moved unchanged (path reshape only). Engine copies; verify by file count + checksum.
four_bucket_rules.Transform: reshape required + automated verification (e.g. index regeneration, schema bump). Engine transforms; verify by re-derivation or schema validation.
four_bucket_rules.Flag: needs human review before placement (ambiguous ownership, possible duplicate). Engine stages to WAI-Harness/spoke/.flagged/ and lists for sign-off; NOT placed automatically.
four_bucket_rules.Drop: provably dead data. Engine NEVER deletes — moves to ~/projects/trash_bin/ preserving relative path (per WSL standing rule), requires explicit --confirm-drop sign-off. Legacy WAI-Spoke/ source drops only on green certification.

no_orphan_invariant: Every live data category present under WAI-Spoke/ at migration time MUST be claimed by exactly one row in home_map below (or explicitly listed in drop_candidates/flag_candidates). The migration engine enumerates the live tree, diffs against this map, and FAILS the dry-run if any category is unclaimed. Coverage is asserted, not assumed.

adoption_scenarios.upgrade: an existing v3 WheelWright spoke → v4. Has WAI-Spoke/ data to reshape via the home map.
adoption_scenarios.bootstrap: a fresh greenfield spoke from the MyWheel blueprint (cold-start, empty local/).
adoption_scenarios.adopt: apply WheelWright to an existing NON-WheelWright project. The value-prop scenario: survey the repo, expose gaps against the harness capability bar, and emit an ALIGNMENT ROADMAP (initiatives + lugs), not just a report. Seeds from the wai-harness-adopt skill.
adoption_scenarios.rule: Every scenario that has existing code/assets (adopt AND upgrade — and bootstrap if the repo is non-empty) BEGINS with the Historian lay-of-the-land survey (below). Never assume the ground is level — a v3 spoke is surveyed like any brownfield project.

historian_lay_of_the_land.principle: ALWAYS survey before migrating or aligning — even a v3 spoke. Don't assume prior alignment; uncover the actual state, then build the plan to bring it together.
historian_lay_of_the_land.owner: Historian (spoke advisor) — extends its retrospective-analysis role with a project/asset survey capability
what_it_reviews: codebase: structure, modules, execution flows (via GitNexus where available), test presence + coverage, dead code
assets: docs, configs, schemas, existing lugs/teachings/state, what is tracked vs untracked
gaps against the harness capability bar (CapabilitiesGraph mandated/recommended), verification coverage, undocumented decisions, drift
for a v3 spoke specifically: stale ghosts, resolved-but-open epics, spec-health drift, untested impl lugs, deprecated paths still in use
historian_lay_of_the_land.output: a survey report (lay-of-the-land) → feeds (1) the gap list, (2) the four-bucket classification for migration, (3) the alignment roadmap (initiatives + lugs prioritized to close gaps). The migration plan is gap-aware, not a blind file move.
historian_lay_of_the_land.always_rule: the engine refuses to --execute upgrade/adopt until a current Historian survey exists for the target (survey is a precondition, recorded + observable).
historian_lay_of_the_land.completeness_audit: the survey can miss things or misclassify (review MED: no completeness guarantee). Two guards: (1) the survey reports its OWN coverage — what it scanned vs what it could not (unscanned dirs, unparseable files) as explicit null/unknown entries, never silent omission; (2) a secondary check (a second detector pass OR a cross-reference against the file manifest + GitNexus index) confirms the survey enumerated the full tree before its output feeds coverage-review/gap-analysis. Survey output carries a coverage_pct + an unscanned[] list; downstream steps see the gaps in the survey itself, not a false 'complete'.

warmup_sequence.principle: Adoption/upgrade follows a strictly ORDERED warmup. You cannot assess coverage until you know what the codebase IS — so the Historian survey runs FIRST and every later step consumes its map. Steps do not reorder.
steps: 1. Historian lay-of-the-land survey — review codebase + assets, produce the survey report (the map). ALWAYS first, even for a v3 spoke. BOOTSTRAP-SAFE (external review A2 deadlock fix): on greenfield/adopt the full Historian advisor is not deployed until step 6, so step 1 runs a lightweight, zero-dependency standalone bootstrap script (historian_core.py — basic filesystem/asset scan) that produces the baseline map and unblocks steps 2-5 BEFORE full advisor deployment. The full Historian later enriches it.

2. Test/verification coverage review — assess what is verified AGAINST the map from step 1 (untested modules, missing e2e, null rate). Cannot run before step 1; it needs the map to know what 'covered' should mean.
3. Gap analysis — survey (step 1) + coverage (step 2) + CapabilitiesGraph bar → the consolidated gap list.
4. Alignment roadmap — gaps (step 3) → prioritized initiatives + lugs (authored to the object-quality standard, spec-object-quality-v4-v1).
5. Migration plan / bootstrap — four-bucket classification (upgrade) or blueprint layout (bootstrap/adopt), gap-aware from steps 1-4.
7. Report certification status to hub registry.
warmup_sequence.ordering_gate: step 2 (coverage review) HALTS if step 1 (survey) has not produced a current report; step 3 HALTS without both 1 and 2. The sequence is enforced, not advisory.

home_map.note: spoke-owned; reshape to timeline structure optional
Ozi live identity; must survive migration
consolidate all Ozi state under ozi/; regenerated artifacts
per spec-storage-layer-v1 + gate topology; flow-defs managed, events per-owner
spoke-owned; drafts route to hub for Trainer curation
ephemeral turn buffers; safe to drop on migration
verify active vs stale; telemetry+model-usage likely Drop after review

home_map.category: ROOT audit .md (ARCHITECTURE-REVIEW, HUB-CONTENT-AUDIT, PUBLIC-PRIVATE-CUTOVER, REPO-MOVE-MAP, REPO-VALUE-AUDIT, SECURITY-AUDIT, TRACK-CHAIN-*)
home_map.note: stale audit artifacts; move to trash after sign-off

home_map.category: ROOT canonical docs (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, WAI-Guide.md, capability-registry-canonical.md, WAI-Lug-Schema-Spec.md)

home_map.note: harness config; never write secret values
Ozi state extension; co-locate with WAI-State.json
state template; confirm canonical vs duplicate
v4 storage-layer DB + migrations; reconcile with spec-storage-layer-v1 (new S45)
historical dead-zone reconstruction artifact, NOT the live track (CLAUDE.md); flag then drop

framework_role_dissolution.principle: v4 has no 'framework' authority and no 'framework' repo. The harness master is MyWheel/WAI-Harness/. Framework's responsibilities redistribute; authorship is democratized (any spoke proposes) but canonicalization centralizes (Trainer curates, MyWheel hosts, Basher distributes).

redistribution.status: becomes managed/; every spoke runs identical copy

redistribution.responsibility: Infrastructure automation (teaching base-cuts, nightly consolidation: teaching_delivery_cut.py, base_cut_draft.py)
redistribution.status: move to hub infra; runs nightly at hub, not per-spoke

redistribution.v4_owner: any spoke (proposes) → Trainer canonicalizes → HUB distributes
redistribution.status: authorship democratized; authority centralized at hub

redistribution.responsibility: Lug routing semantics, dispatch scoring (lug_utils, spoke_expediter, ozi_autopilot)
redistribution.v4_owner: managed/ code run by each spoke's Ozi
redistribution.status: shared orchestration; no central owner needed

replacement.HUB: infrastructure, base-kit cuts, fleet-wide harness upgrades, nightly consolidation
replacement.TRAINER: curation / teaching-canonicalization / broadly-useful pattern review (bubble-up evolution proposals)

routed_to_deprecation.teaching_required: deprecation-requires-teaching-v1 — a deprecation teaching MUST accompany the routed_to:FRAMEWORK retirement (no silent retirement). Legacy lugs carrying routed_to:FRAMEWORK are treated as legacy-mode by Trainer (functional, not rejected) and rerouted per this map.

constraints: No-orphan invariant is a HARD GATE: dry-run fails if any live category is unmapped
Migration is ADDITIVE: build WAI-Harness/ alongside WAI-Spoke/; legacy source is never deleted until green certification + sign-off
Flag and Drop buckets require explicit human sign-off; engine never auto-deletes
Drop = move to ~/projects/trash_bin/ preserving relative path (WSL standing rule), never rm
managed/ is agent-read-only post-migration; only Basher writes it
Ozi state consolidates under spoke/ozi/ — not a generic advisor folder
routed_to:FRAMEWORK retirement requires an accompanying deprecation teaching (no silent retirement)
Secret values / .env contents are never written into any migrated file
tests: No-orphan: enumerate live WAI-Spoke/ categories, diff against home_map → zero unmapped (dry-run asserts this; injecting a synthetic unmapped folder makes the dry-run FAIL)

Bucket routing: a Preserve category copies unchanged (checksum match); a Transform category re-derives and schema-validates; a Flag category stages to .flagged/ and is NOT auto-placed; a Drop category moves to trash_bin only with --confirm-drop
Additive: after dry-run AND after execute, WAI-Spoke/ source still exists intact (nothing deleted pre-certification)
Ozi consolidation: WAI-State.json + ozi-brief.json + advisors/ozi/* all land under spoke/ozi/
managed read-only: a write attempt to spoke/managed/ by a non-Basher actor is flagged
routed_to: a lug with routed_to:FRAMEWORK is detected as legacy and rerouted to HUB/TRAINER/BASHER per the map; deprecation teaching exists
Coverage report: dry-run emits a per-category report (claimed/bucket/target/volume) summing to the full live tree

open_questions: historian/cache.db + telemetry/ + model-usage/: confirm regenerable/stale before Drop
corpus-tests/ + advisors/advisor_manager.py/evolution_engine.py: managed (harness) or local (spoke) — confirm during dry-run Flag review
Does the framework repo physically rename to a harness-source spoke, or keep its name with the authority dissolved? (proposed: keep name, dissolve authority)

perceive: Read this spec + the S46 audit in architecture_harness_v4.md. Enumerate the live WAI-Spoke/ tree + repo root. Confirm impl-v4-harness-skeleton-v1 status (skeleton must exist before engine runs).
execute: Implemented via impl-v4-harness-skeleton-v1 (build WAI-Harness/ skeleton + manifest) then impl-v4-migration-engine-v1 (tools/v4_migrate.py reading this map). Author the deprecation-requires-teaching for routed_to:FRAMEWORK. Deliver hub-directed items (infra absorption, routed_to HUB/TRAINER) and Basher-directed items (managed/ distribution) as lugs to their incoming/.
verify: Run all tests[]. Dry-run produces a complete coverage report with zero orphans; additive property holds; Flag/Drop require sign-off.

home_map_completions.note: closes lug-v4-home-map-completion-v1; engine dry-run now zero-orphan
